$schema-comment: Dev-forge-compatible upstream tracking schema. `origin` describes the immediate upstream (dev-forge); `lineage` records prior layers for attribution. `customizations[]` describes local edits relative to upstream paths inside `origin.path`. Local files live at `plugins/clean-code-ts/skills/improve-codebase-architecture/<target>`. Compatible with forge-keeper:update-check (or any equivalent tool).

local_layout.note: Local skill dir name matches upstream skill name so update-check can map upstream files to local files 1:1. User-facing skill name is overridden in SKILL.md frontmatter (`name: clean-code-ts`).

customizations.summary: Renamed skill (frontmatter `name`) from improve-codebase-architecture to clean-code-ts; rewrote `description` to trigger on TypeScript projects (mentions tsconfig.json, .ts/.tsx).
customizations.reason: Makes the skill auto-activate on TS work and signals its TS focus in the marketplace listing.

customizations.summary: Added `## TypeScript focus` section enumerating TS idioms (discriminated unions, branded types, `unknown` over `any`, `Readonly<T>`, string unions over `enum`, TS `interface` for ports). Added TS-specific friction signals to the Explore step.
customizations.reason: The upstream skill is intentionally language-agnostic; this plugin is the TS-focused fork.

customizations.summary: Removed cross-links to sibling forge-mattpocock skills (`../grill-with-docs/GLOSSARY-FORMAT.md`, `../grill-with-docs/ADR-FORMAT.md`); replaced with inline guidance.
customizations.reason: This plugin ships only the architecture skill, so the cross-skill links would be broken.

customizations.summary: Added `## TypeScript idioms for designing interfaces` with TS code examples: discriminated unions, branded types, `unknown` at boundaries, option-object signatures, ports as TS `interface`, avoiding `enum`, readonly-by-default.
customizations.reason: Concretes the upstream's interface-design advice with TS-specific examples.

customizations.summary: Annotated each dependency category (1–4) with a `TypeScript flavour` paragraph; added `expectTypeOf`/`assertType` note to the testing strategy.
customizations.reason: Same as custom-04, applied to deepening mechanics.

customizations.summary: Added `## TypeScript naming conventions` section (vacuous nouns to avoid, verbs vs nouns, earned suffixes like Repository/Gateway/Policy, case conventions). Extended `## Rejected framings` with TS-specific rejections (`enum` over string unions, `any` instead of `unknown`).
customizations.reason: Names are part of the interface; the upstream vocabulary needed TS-specific extensions.

customizations.summary: Copied verbatim from upstream — no TS adaptation needed.
